title: asclepias cucullata subsp. scabrifolia
intro: Asclepias cucullata subsp. scabrifolia, also known as the rough-leaved milkweed, is a plant species within the Asclepias genus that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce vomiting and purging, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, and its health benefits are attributed to its high concentration of cardenolides and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried, powdered, and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to leverage its medicinal properties.
